title: eremophila jucunda subsp. jucunda
intro: Eremophila jucunda subsp. jucunda is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, commonly known as the Western Australian emu bush or lemon-scented emu bush. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and providing protection against oxidative stress. Eremophila jucunda subsp. jucunda can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues.
